title: mentha × villosa var. lamarckii
intro: Mentha × villosa var. lamarckii, also known as 'Lamarck's Catmint' or 'Lemon Catmint', is a variety of hybrid mint plant that is a cross between Mentha villosa and Mentha piperita, characterized by its distinctive minty aroma and lemon-scented leaves. The plant contains menthol, menthone, and limonene, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, including its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural antiseptic. The health benefits of Mentha × villosa var. lamarckii include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve respiratory health, and aid in the treatment of headaches and fever. Mentha × villosa var. lamarckii can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used as a flavoring agent in culinary recipes and as a natural remedy in aromatherapy.
